cards.title: Naturopathic History Taking — Complete Framework
cards._legacyHtml:
Naturopathic History = SOAP + VITALS + DIET + MIND + SPIRIT
A comprehensive naturopathic history takes 45–60 min for new patients. Every symptom has a physical, mental, emotional, and spiritual component. Never rush this — it IS the diagnosis.
1
Chief Complaint & History Duration, onset, character, radiation, aggravating/relieving factors, timing. WHAT makes it better? WHAT makes it worse? WHAT was happening in life when it started? (Emotional triggers are often the true cause.)
2
Dietary History (24-hr recall) What did you eat yesterday — breakfast, lunch, dinner, snacks, drinks? Water intake? Cooking method? Food intolerances? Emotional eating? Eating times? Eating speed? Note: meal timing affects circadian biology significantly.
3
Sleep History Hours/night, sleep onset time, wake time, dream recall, night sweats, snoring, restlessness, nocturia, refreshed on waking? Insomnia pattern: onset (anxiety), maintenance (hormonal, pain), early morning (depression).
4
Bowel & Digestive History Frequency, Bristol stool type (1–7), colour, urgency, bloating, flatulence, reflux, nausea. Bowel frequency: 1–3x/day normal. Type 1–2 = constipation; Type 6–7 = diarrhoea. Last colonoscopy? Blood PR?
5
Stress & Emotional History Current stressors (work, relationships, finances, health anxiety). Trauma history (ACEs — adverse childhood experiences). Coping mechanisms. Support system. Purpose and meaning. Spirituality/faith.
6
Exercise & Movement Type, frequency, duration, intensity. Sedentary time. Occupation (sitting vs standing). Recreational activity. Previous sporting history.
7
Toxic Load Assessment Smoking (pack-years), alcohol (units/week), recreational drugs, caffeine (cups/day), occupational chemical exposure, medications (OTC + prescribed + herbal), air quality at home/work.
🟡 Pearl — The First Question
"What do you think is causing your problem?" — Ask this first, always. Patients often know the cause (stress, diet, a specific event) but feel it won't be taken seriously. This question builds rapport, saves time, and reveals the patient's health belief model — which determines compliance with your recommendations.

cards.title: Prakriti Assessment — Ayurvedic Constitution
cards._legacyHtml:
Prakriti = Individual Constitutional Type
Determined at conception — unchangeable. Governs susceptibility to disease, drug response, optimal diet and lifestyle. Vikriti = current imbalanced state (what brings patient to clinic).
FeatureVata (Air+Space)Pitta (Fire+Water)Kapha (Earth+Water)
Body frameThin, bony, variableMedium, muscularLarge, stocky, gains easily
SkinDry, rough, coolOily, warm, reddish, prone to rashThick, oily, pale, cool
HairDry, brittle, frizzyFine, oily, early greying/baldnessThick, oily, lustrous
DigestionVariable, bloating, gasStrong, hyperacid, refluxSlow, heavy, nausea
BowelsConstipation, dry, hardLoose, frequent, diarrhoea-proneSlow, regular, formed
SleepLight, interrupted, insomniaModerate, vivid dreams, wakes hotDeep, prolonged, hard to wake
Mind/emotionAnxious, creative, fearful, quickSharp, competitive, irritable, focusedCalm, loyal, possessive, slow to anger
Climate preferenceHates cold/wind, loves warmthHates heat, loves coolHates cold/damp, loves dry heat
Disease tendencyJoint pain, nervous disorders, constipation, insomniaInflammation, ulcers, liver disease, skinObesity, T2DM, respiratory, sinusitis
Pulse qualityThin, irregular, rapid (snake)Moderate, strong, bounding (frog)Slow, deep, steady (swan)
🟡 Clinical Application
Most patients are dual constitution (Vata-Pitta, Pitta-Kapha). Pure single types are rare. Identify the PRIMARY dosha — this guides diet, hydrotherapy temperature choice, massage oil, and herb selection. Vata → warm oil massage, warm water therapy, grounding foods. Pitta → cool hydrotherapy, anti-inflammatory diet, bitter herbs. Kapha → vigorous dry massage, hot/cold contrast therapy, light stimulating diet, pungent herbs.

cards.title: Tongue & Pulse Diagnosis — Clinical Basics
cards._legacyHtml:
ParameterFindingSignificance
Tongue — ColourPale pink (normal)Healthy blood and Qi
Pale/whiteBlood deficiency, anaemia, cold
Red/crimsonHeat excess, yin deficiency, fever
Purple/duskyBlood stagnation, circulatory issue
Tongue — CoatThin white (normal)Healthy stomach function
Thick whiteCold, damp accumulation, SIBO
Thick yellowHeat, infection, liver/GB stress
No coat (peeled)Yin/fluid deficiency, chronic illness
Tongue — ShapeSwollen/scalloped edgesSpleen Qi deficiency, dampness
Thin/narrowBlood/yin deficiency
Cracks (midline)Stomach yin deficiency, chronic stress
Pulse — Rate60–100 bpm (normal)Healthy heart rate
Pulse — QualityWiry/tautLiver Qi stagnation, stress, pain
Slippery/rollingDampness/phlegm, pregnancy
Thin/threadyBlood/yin deficiency, anaemia
Deep/sinkingInterior condition, kidney deficiency
🟡 Integration Pearl
Tongue diagnosis is more reliable than pulse for beginners — the tongue doesn't change with practitioner pressure or patient anxiety. Always examine in natural light (not fluorescent). Ask patient not to eat, drink coffee/tea, or brush tongue for 1 hour before. Most significant finding: tongue body colour reflects chronic constitution; tongue coat reflects current digestive state.
